name: 李小冬
current_positions: Founder, Chairman and CEO of Sea Limited
Chairman of Lion City Sailors FC
President of the Football Association of Singapore (FAS)
Member of the National University of Singapore Board of Trustees
Independent Non-Executive Director of Shangri-La Asia Limited

work_experience.position: Founder, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer
Chairman (acquired the club, formerly Home United, first privatized S-League club)

person_relationships.description: Co-founder of Sea Limited; serves as COO; China-born Carnegie Mellon graduate; fellow billionaire (net worth ~US$4.8 billion)
Co-founder of Sea Limited; fellow billionaire; China-born, came to Singapore as a teenager

family_members.industry_or_organization: Met at Stanford; lives in Singapore with Li; wife reportedly purchased a GCB near Botanic Gardens for S$42.5 million

major_achievements.achievement: Founded Garena as a digital entertainment and game publishing company in a single shophouse off Maxwell Road, Singapore
Launched Shopee, which grew to become one of the leading e-commerce platforms in Southeast Asia
Rebranded Garena to Sea Limited after raising US$550 million in a funding round; listed on NYSE later that year
Became a billionaire when Sea Ltd share price increased 45%, pushing net worth above US$1 billion
Appeared on the cover of Forbes Asia magazine
Acquired Home United FC (renamed Lion City Sailors), the first privatization of a Singapore Premier League club
Opened a 28,000 sq meter, S$10 million training facility in Singapore for youth football development
Elected unopposed as President of FAS; pledged to expand youth development pathways for local players

biography_summary: Forrest Li Xiaodong (born 1977 or 1978, Tianjin, China) is a Singaporean entrepreneur and billionaire (net worth ~US$3.6 billion as of 2024). He earned a BA in Engineering from Shanghai Jiao Tong University and an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business, where Steve Jobs' commencement speech inspired him to become a startup founder. After moving to Singapore with limited resources and S$100,000 in student debt, he founded Garena (later Sea Limited) in 2009. Under his leadership, Sea has grown into Southeast Asia's largest internet company with three main businesses: Garena (digital entertainment), Shopee (e-commerce), and SeaMoney/Monee (digital financial services). Sea went public on the NYSE in 2017. He is also Chairman of Lion City Sailors FC (acquired 2020) and was elected President of the Football Association of Singapore in 2025. He is married (met wife at Stanford) and lives in Singapore. He also serves on the NUS Board of Trustees and as an independent director of Shangri-La Asia Limited.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with detailed career timeline, co-founder relationships (Gang Ye, David Chen), football involvement, Stanford inspiration story, and current board positions. Wife name not publicly disclosed. Birth date is approximate (1977 or 1978 per Wikipedia). Net worth ~US$3.6B per Forbes 2024.
